Quality Education.reason: Course promotes understanding of trade rules, relevant to Target 4.7 ('knowledge for sustainable development').
evidence: understanding the future development of the service trade rules

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Course discusses service trade's economic impact, linking to Target 8.3 ('support productive activities').
evidence: 服務貿易占今日全球貿易比重近百分之五十

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Course addresses service trade regulations, relevant to Target 9.2 ('sustainable industrialization').

No Poverty.reason: The course covers "Financial Services," which has a tangential link to Target 1.4 regarding access to economic resources, though the primary focus is on international regulation.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course analyzes rules for services, which are over two-thirds of global GDP, directly supporting Target 8.1 (economic growth) and 8.10 (financial services).
evidence: 服務的產值逾全球國民生產毛額的三分之二

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course explicitly analyzes the regulation of key infrastructure sectors like "Telecoms" and "Transportation," directly addressing Target 9.1 (resilient infrastructure) and 9.c (access to ICT).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The study of WTO law inherently involves Target 10.a (special treatment for developing countries) and Target 10.5 through its analysis of global "Financial Services" regulation.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course covers "Transportation" services, a key component for developing sustainable transport systems under Target 11.2, by examining its international regulatory framework.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course is a deep study of the WTO/GATS, a rules-based international institution, directly aligning with Target 16.3 (rule of law) and 16.8 (global governance).
evidence: Learning the legal perspectives of service trade

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course is fundamentally about the "multilateral trading system under the World Trade Organization," which is the explicit subject of Target 17.10.
evidence: General Agreement on Trade in Services
